questions.text:
If $\text{A}$ is a skew symmetric matrix then $\text{A}^t$  is

  1. Diagonal matrix 
  2. $\text{A}$
  3. $0$
  4. $-\text{A}$

Let $A$ and $B$ be any two arbitrary events, then, which one of the following is TRUE?

  1. $P (A \cap B) = P(A)P(B)$
  2. $P (A \cup B) = P(A)+P(B)$
  3. $P (A \mid B) = P(A \cap B)P(B)$
  4. $P (A \cup B) \leq P(A) + P(B)$

Using Newton-Raphson method, a root correct to 3 decimal places of  $x^3 - 3x -5 = 0$

  1. 2.222
  2. 2.275
  3. 2.279
  4. None of the above

What does a data dictionary will identify?

  1. Field name
  2. Field format
  3. Field types
  4. All of the above

Which of the following concurrency control protocol ensures both conflict and free from deadlock? ,

  1. Time stamp ordering
  2. 2 Phase locking
  3. Both (a) and (b)
  4. None of the above

ACID properties of a transactions are

  1. Atomicity, consistency, isolation, database
  2. Atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability
  3. Atomicity, consistency, integrity, durability
  4. Atomicity, consistency, integrity, database

$$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|l|} \hline \textbf{Employee} & \textbf{Department} & \textbf{OT_allowance} \\ \hline \text{RAMA} & \text{Mechanical} & \text{5000} \\\hline \text{GOPI} & \text{Electrical} & \text{2000} \\\hline \text{SINDHU} & \text{Computer} & \text{4000} \\\hline \text{MAHESH} & \text{Civil} & \text{1500} \\\hline \end{array}$$

What is the output of the following SQL query?

select count(*) from 
    ((select Employee, Department from Overtime_allowance) 
    natural join 
    (select Department, OT_allowance from Overtime_allowance) 
    as T);
  1. $16$
  2. $4$
  3. $8$
  4. None of the above

Which symbol denote derived attributes in ER Model?

  1. Double ellipse
  2. Dashed ellipse
  3. Squared ellipse
  4. Ellipse with attribute name underlined

The problem $\text{3-SAT}$ and $\text{2-SAT}$ are 

  1. both in $\text{P}$

  2. both $\text{NP}$ complete

  3. $\text{NP}$-complete and in $\text{P}$ respectively

  4. undecidable and $\text{NP}$ complete respectively

Given the following statements

  • S1 : Every context-sensitive language $L$ is recursive
  • S2 : There exists a recursive language that is not context-sensitive


Which statements are true?

  1. Only S1 is correct
  2. Only S2 is correct
  3. Both S1 and S2 are not correct
  4. Both S1 and S2 are correct

Which one of the following is FALSE?

  1. There is a unique minimal DFA for every regular language

  2. Every NFA can be converted to an equivalent PDA.

  3. Complement of every context-free language is recursive.

  4. Every nondeterministic PDA can be converted to an equivalent deterministic PDA.

In some programming language, an identifier is permitted to be a letter followed by any number of letters or digits. If $L$ and $D$ denote the sets of letters and digits respectively, which of the following expressions defines an identifier?

  1. $(L + D)^+$
  2. $(L.D)^*$
  3. $L(L + D)^*$
  4. $L(L.D)^*$

The recurrence relation that arises in relation with the complexity of binary search is:

  1. $T(n) = 2T\left(\frac{n}{2}\right)+k, \text{ k is a constant }$

  2. $T(n) = T\left(\frac{n}{2}\right)+k, \text{ k is a constant }$

  3. $T(n) = T\left(\frac{n}{2}\right)+\log n$

  4. $T(n) = T\left(\frac{n}{2}\right)+n$

Which one of the following in-place sorting algorithms needs the minimum number of swaps?

  1. Insertion Sort
  2. Quick Sort
  3. Heap Sort
  4. Selection Sort

Given two statements

  1. Insertion of an element should be done at the last node of the circular list
  2. Deletion of an element should be done at the last node of the circular list
  1. Both are true
  2. Both are false
  3. First is false and second is true
  4. None of the above

Which of the following data structure is useful in traversing a given graph by breadth first search?

  1. Stack
  2. Queue
  3. List
  4. None of the above

How many $128\times 8$ bit RAMs are required to design $32\;\text{K}\times 32$ bit RAM?

  1. $512$
  2. $1024$
  3. $128$
  4. $32$

The most appropriate matching for the following pairs :

$$\begin{array}{clcl} \text{X.} & \text{Indirect Addressing} & \text{i.} & \text{Loop} \\ \text{Y.} & \text{Immediate Addressing} & \text{ii.} & \text{Pointers} \\ \text{Z.} & \text{Auto Decrement Addressing} & \text{iii.} & \text{Constants} \\ \end{array}$$

  1. $\text{X-iii, Y-ii, Z-i}$
  2. $\text{X-ii, Y-iii, Z-i}$
  3. $\text{X-iii, Y-i, Z-ii}$
  4. $\text{X-ii, Y-i, Z-iii}$

Which interrupt in $8085$ Microprocessor is unmaskable?

  1. $\textsf{RST 5.5}$
  2. $\textsf{RST 7.5}$
  3. $\textsf{TRAP}$
  4. Both (a) and (b)

A cache memory needs an access time of $30$ ns and main memory $150$ ns, what is average access time of CPU (assume hit ratio $= 80\%)?$

  1. $60$ ns
  2. $30$ ns
  3. $150$ ns
  4. $70$ ns

Which one of the following Boolean expressions is NOT a tautology?

  1. $((a \rightarrow b) \wedge (b \rightarrow c)) \rightarrow  (a \rightarrow c)$
  2. $(a \leftrightarrow c) \rightarrow (\sim b\rightarrow (a\wedge c))$
  3. $(a\wedge b \wedge c)\rightarrow (c \vee a)$
  4. $a\rightarrow (b\rightarrow a)$

What is the minimum number of two-input $\text{NAND}$ gates used to perform the function of two-input $\text{OR}$ gate?

  1. One
  2. Two
  3. Three
  4. Four

When two $n$-bit binary numbers are added the sum will contain at the most

  1. $n$ bits
  2. $n + 2$ bits
  3. $n + 3$ bits
  4. $n + 1$ bits

The 2-input XOR has a high output only when the input values are
(a) low
(b) high

(c) same

(d) different

Advantage of synchronous sequential circuits over asynchronous one is :

  1. Lower hardware requirement
  2. Better noise immunity
  3. Faster operation
  4. All of the above

Physical topology of FDDI is?

  1. Bus
  2. Ring
  3. Star
  4. None of the above

In networking terminology UTP means

  1. Uniquitous teflon port
  2. Uniformly terminating port
  3. Unshielded twisted pair
  4. Unshielded T-connector port

The default subnet mask for a class B network can be

  1. $255.255.255.0$
  2. $255.0.0.0$
  3. $255.255.192.0$
  4. $255.255.0.0$

If there are $n$ devices (nodes) in a network, what is the number of cable links required for a fully connected mesh and a star topology respectively

  1. $n(n-1)/2$, $n-1$
  2. $n$, $n-1$
  3. $n-1$, $n$
  4. $n-1$,  $n(n -1)/ 2$

Which of the following protocol is used for transferring electronic mail messages from one machine to another?

  1. TELNET
  2. FTP
  3. SNMP
  4. SMTP

Which media access control protocol is used by $\textsf{IEEE 802.11}$ wireless LAN?

  1. CDMA
  2. CSMA/CA
  3. ALOHA
  4. None of the above

An Ethernet frame that is  less than the $\textsf{IEEE 802.3}$ minimum length of $64$ octets is called

  1. Short frame
  2. Small frame
  3. Mini frame
  4. Runt frame

Match with the suitable one :

$$\begin{array} {clcl} & \textbf{List-1} & & \textbf{List-2} \\ \text{(A)} & \text{Multicast group membership }& \text{1.} & \text{Distance Vector routing} \\ \text{(B)} & \text{Interior gateway protocol}& \text{2.} & \text{IGMP} \\ \text{(C)} & \text{Exterior gateway protocol}& \text{3.} & \text{OSPF} \\ \text{(D)} & \text{RIP}& \text{4.} & \text{BGP} \\ \end{array}$$

  1. $\text{A-2, B-3, C-4, D-1}$
  2. $\text{A-2, B-4, C-3, D-1}$
  3. $\text{A-3, B-4, C-1, D-2}$
  4. $\text{A-3, B-1, C-4, D-2}$

$\text{MD5}$ is a widely used hash function for producing hash value of

  1. $64$ bits
  2. $128$ bits
  3. $512$ bits
  4. $1024$ bits

Which protocol suite designed by IETF to provide security for a packet at the Internet layer?

  1. IPSec
  2. NetSec
  3. PacketSec
  4. SSL

Pretty Good Privacy (PGP) is used in:

  1. Browser security
  2. FTP security
  3. Email security
  4. None of the above

What is WPA?

  1. wired protected access
  2. wi-fi protected access
  3. wired process access
  4. wi-fi process access

Estimation at software development effort for organic software in basic COCOMO is:

  1. E = 2.0 (KLOC) 1.05  PM
  2. E = 3.4 (KLOC) 1.06  PM
  3. E = 2.4 (KLOC) 1.05  PM
  4. E = 2.4 (KLOC) 1.07  PM

XPath is used to navigate through elements and attributes in

  1. XSL document
  2. XML document
  3. XHTML document
  4. XQuery document

What is the output of the C++ program?
 

#include <iostream>
using namespace std;

void square(int *x){
    *x = (*x)++ * (*x);
}

void square(int *x, int *y){
    *x = (*x) * --(*y);
}

int main()
{
  int number = 30;
  square(&number, &number);
  cout<<number;
  return 0;
}
  1. 910
  2. 920
  3. 870
  4. 900

Which of the following operator(s) cannot be overloaded?

  1. .(member Access or Dot operator)
  2. ?: (ternary or Conditional Operator)
  3. :: ( Scope Resolution Operator)
  4. All of the above

Which of the following UML 2.0 diagrams capture behavioral aspects of a system?

  1. Use case diagram, Object diagram, Activity diagram and state machine diagram
  2. Use case diagram, Activity diagram and state machine diagram
  3. Object diagram, Communication Diagram, Timing diagram and Interaction diagram
  4. Object diagram, Composite structure diagram, package diagram and Deployment diagram

Which of the following is associated with objects?

  1. State
  2. Behavior
  3. Identity
  4. All of the above

Which of these is characteristic of $\textsf{RAID 5}?$

  1. Dedicated parity
  2. Double parity
  3. Hamming code parity
  4. Distributed parity

SATA is the abbreviation of

  1. Serial Advanced Technology Attachment
  2. Serial Advanced Technology Architecture
  3. Serial Advanced Technology Adapter
  4. Serial Advanced Technology Array

Capability maturity Model (CMM) is the methodology to

  1. develop and refine an organization's software development process
  2. develop the software
  3. test the software
  4. All of the above

What problem is solved by Dijikstra banker' algorithm?

  1. Mutual exclusion
  2. Deadlock recovery
  3. Deadlock avoidance
  4. Cache coherence

The number of swappings needed to sort the numbers $8 , 22, 7, 9, 31, 5, 13$ in ascending order using bubble sort is

  1. $11$
  2. $12$
  3. $13$
  4. $10$

If the post order traversal gives ab -cd * + then the label of the nodes 1,2,3.. will be

  1. + , -, *, a,b,c,d
  2. a, -,b,+,c,*,d
  3. a,b,c,d,-,*,+
  4. -,a,b,+,*,c,d

Wha is the output of the following program?

main()
{
    int a = 10;
    if(fork()) == 0))
        a++;
    printf("%d\n",a);  
}
  1. 10 and 11
  2. 10
  3. 11
  4. 11 and 11

Given reference to the following pages by a program

0,9,0,1,8,1,8,7,8,7,1,2,8,2,7,8,2,3,8,3

How many page faults will occur if the program has three page frames available to it and uses an optimal replacement?

  1. 7
  2. 8
  3. 9
  4. None of the above

In a doubly linked list the number of pointers affected for an insertion operation will be

  1. 4
  2. 0
  3. 1
  4. Depends on the nodes of doubly linked list

Consider the following function

void swap(int a, int b)  
{       
    int temp;
    temp = a;
    a = b;
    b = temp;  
}

In order to exchange the values of two variables $x$ and $y$.

  1. call $swap(x, y)$

  2. call $swap(\&x, \&y)$

  3. $swap (x, y)$ cannot be used as it does not return any value

  4. $swap (x, y)$ cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value

What does the following C-statement declare?

int (*f) (int * );

  1. A function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns an integer

  2. A function that takes an integer as argument and returns an integer pointer

  3. A pointer to a function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns an integer

  4. A function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns a function pointer

Mutual exclusion problem occurs

  1. between two disjoint processes that do not interact
  2. among processes that share resources
  3. among processes that do not use the same resource
  4. between two processes that uses different resources of different machine

$(1217)_8$ is equivalent to

  1. $(1217)_{16}$
  2. $(028F)_{16}$
  3. $(2297)_{10}$
  4. $(0B17)_{16}$

Which of the following is not a life cycle model?

  1. Spiral model
  2. Prototyping model
  3. Waterfall model
  4. Capability maturity model

The best data structure to check whether an arithmetic expression has  balanced parenthesis is  a:

  1. Queue
  2. Stack
  3. Tree
  4. List

The cyclomatic complexity of each of the modules $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$ shown below is $10.$ What is the cyclomatic complexity of the sequential integration shown on the right hand side?

  1. $19$
  2. $21$
  3. $20$
  4. $10$

In software maintenance tackling the changes in the hardware or software environment where the software works, is

  1. Corrective maintenance
  2. Perfective maintenance
  3. Adaptive maintenance
  4. Preventive maintenance

What will be the output of the following C code?

#include <stdio.h>
main()
{
    int i;
    for(i=0;i<5;i++)
    {
        int i=10;
        printf("%d" , i);
        i++;
    }
    return 0;
}
  1. 10 11 12 13 14
  2. 10 10 10 10 10
  3. 0 1 2 3 4
  4. Compilation error

What does the following program do when the input is unsigned 16 bit integer?

#include<stdio.h>
main(){
  unsigned int num;
  int i;
  scanf("%u", &num);
  for(i=0;i<16;i++){
    printf("%d", (num<<i&1<<15)?1:0);
  }
}
  1. It prints all even bits from num
  2. It prints all odd bits from num
  3. It prints binary equivalent of num
  4. None of above

What is the output of the following program?

#include<stdio.h>
int tmp=20;
main()
{
  printf("%d", tmp);
  func();
  printf("%d", tmp);

}
func()
{
 static int tmp=10;
 printf("%d", tmp);
}
  1. 20 10 10
  2. 20 10 20
  3. 20 20 20
  4. 10 10 10

Which product metric gives the measure of the average length of words and sentence in documents?

  1. SCI number
  2. Cyclomatic complexity
  3. LOC
  4. Fog index

Consider the disk system with 100 cylinders. The request to access the cylinders occur in the following sequence.

4, 37, 10,7,19,73,2,15,6,20

Assuming the head is currently at cylinder 50 what is the time taken to satisfy all requests if it takes 1 ms to move from one cylinder to adjacent one and shortest seek time first algorithm is used.

  1. 95 ms
  2. 119 ms
  3. 233 ms
  4. 276 ms

A B-Tree used as an index for a large database table has four levels including the root node. If a new key is inserted in this index, then the maximum number of nodes that could be newly created in the process are

  1. $5$
  2. $4$
  3. $3$
  4. $2$

A critical region

  1. is a piece of code which only one process executes at a time
  2. is a region prone to deadlock
  3. is a piece of code which only a finite number of processes execute
  4. is found only in windows NT operating system

Choose the equivalent prefix form of the following expression

(a+(b-c))*((d-e)/(f+g-h))

  1. *+a-bc/-de-+fgh
  2. *+a-bc-/de-+fgh
  3. *+a-bc/-ed-+fgh
  4. *+ab-c/-de-+fgh

We use malloc and calloc for:

  1. Dynamic memory allocation
  2. Static memory allocation
  3. Both dynamic memory allocation and static memory allocation
  4. None of these

At a particular time the value of counting semaphore is 10. It will become 7 after:

  1. 3 V operations
  2. 3 P operations
  3. 5 V operations and 2 P operations
  4. 2 V operations and 5 P operations

The Linux command mknod myfifo b 4 16

  1. will create a character device if user is root
  2. will create a named pipe FIFO if user is root
  3. will create a block device if user is root
  4. None of these

Which of the following statement is true?

  1. Hard real time OS has less jitter than soft real time OS
  2. Hard real time OS has more jitter than soft real time OS
  3. Hard real time OS has equal jitter as soft real time OS
  4. None of the above

Which of these is a super class of all errors and exceptions in the Java language?

  1. Runtime Exceptions
  2. Throwable
  3. Catchable
  4. None of the above

Choose the most appropriate HTML tag in the following to create a numbered list

  1. <dl>
  2. <list>
  3. <ul>
  4. <ol>

Which of the following algorithms solves the all pair shortest path problem?

  1. Prim's algorithm
  2. Dijkstra's algorithm
  3. Bellman ford algorithm
  4. Floyd warshalls algorithm

If $L$ and $P$ are two recursively enumerable languages then they are not closed under

  1. Kleene star $L^*$ of $L$
  2. Intersection $L \cap P$
  3. Union $L \cup P$
  4. Set difference

In the context of modular software design, which one of the following combinations is desirable?

  1. High cohesion and high coupling
  2. High cohesion and low coupling
  3. Low cohesion and high coupling
  4. Low cohesion and low coupling

The output of  a lexical analyzer is

  1. A parse tree
  2. Intermediate code
  3. Machine code
  4. A stream of tokens

The time complexity of computing the transitive closure of a binary relation on a set of $n$ elements is known to be

a. $O(n\log n)$

b. $O\left( n^{3/2}\right)$

c. $O( n^3 )$

d. $O(n)$
